title: Is External
text: Is External Determines whether the Field's data can be externalized outside the component, either by storing it in a database or passing it in an entity or occurrence parameter. Value Description T The data of the Component Field can be stored in a database, or it can be exchanged with other components via entity or occurrence parameters. F The Component Field cannot be externalized. Description In most cases, the value of Is External is determined by the Database Behavior property of its parent entity. By default, if an entity's Database Behavior property is T , each of its fields has its Is External property set to T , and vice versa. However, if you are defining a component that needs to exchange information by means of entity or occurrence parameters, the fields to be included in the data exchange must have Is External set to T , even if the parent entity is not a database entity. Use Static or Control fields for buttons and fields containing static data, such as images or labels. In these fields, the field values are retained when the parent component entity is saved, or cleared, or when occurrences are added or deleted. B Field contents are not stored in the database and are not repeated in entity occurrences. X Field contents are not stored in the database, and are not repeated in entity occurrences. In Form components, the normal Uniface checks are not performed for Control fields. The behavior of Static and Control fields is the same in all components except Forms. In Form components, the normal Uniface checks are not performed for Control fields. For example, by defining the Quit button as a Control field, you can leave a component even when some of the fields contain incorrect data. Applies To Field Related Links entity Uniface data type for transferring occurrences of entities between components. occurrence Uniface data type containing a single occurrence of an entity. For use in parameters. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
